hook: You already know how to evaluate sine, cosine, and tangent — but what if you need the exact value of $\sin(75°)$ or need to simplify a messy trig expression without a calculator? Right now, those problems might feel out of reach. In this unit, you'll discover that trigonometric functions follow deep, elegant relationships that turn seemingly impossible problems into ones you can solve with pure reasoning.
concept: Analytic trigonometry is about using known relationships between trig functions — called identities — to rewrite, simplify, and solve expressions and equations exactly. The unifying idea is that every technique here flows from a core set of rules, collectively known as the 'trigonometric identity framework,' that let you transform one form into another with confidence.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn the essential identity toolkit — Pythagorean, reciprocal, and quotient identities — that serves as the foundation for every manipulation in this unit.
Master the art of proving that two trig expressions are equivalent by strategically rewriting one side until it matches the other.
Apply the sum and difference formulas for sine, cosine, and tangent to find exact values like $\cos(75°)$ and simplify expressions involving combined angles.
Use double-angle and half-angle formulas to evaluate trig functions at angles like $\frac{\pi}{8}$ and rewrite expressions in more useful forms.
You will learn to find all exact solutions to trig equations on a given interval — and over all real numbers — by combining algebraic techniques with your identity toolkit.
Apply advanced conversion formulas to transform products of trig functions into sums, and sums into products, unlocking techniques used in physics, engineering, and higher mathematics.
